summary: Verified ADR 0279 from the latest-main integration worktree on top of the synchronized 0.177.104 / 0.130.69 baseline. The exact-main replay exposed and fixed the Docker bridge-chain helper regression, preserved the broader Keycloak and mail-platform recovery hardening, reran the Grist service live apply successfully in r5, re-expanded the shared edge certificate to include grist.lv3.org, and refreshed the integrated platform baseline to 0.130.70 while preserving the earlier first-live Grist proof on 0.130.60.

verification.observed: The synchronized latest-main compatibility slice returned `44 passed` across `tests/test_common_docker_bridge_chains_helper.py`, `tests/test_grist_runtime_role.py`, `tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py`, `tests/test_mail_platform_runtime_role.py`, and `tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py`.

`ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/services/grist.yml --syntax-check`, `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/mail-platform.yml --syntax-check`, and `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/keycloak.yml --syntax-check` all passed from the integration worktree. The governed replay also passed the live-apply contract, standby-capacity check, and immutable-guest-replacement gate once rerun with the documented `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` exception for this stateful edge service.

The exact-main chronology is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-0.177.105.txt`, `...-r2.txt`, `...-r3.txt`, `...-r4.txt`, and the successful `...-r5.txt`. Those runs record the initial worktree-relative SSH key mistake, the generated-portal bootstrap gap, the Docker bridge-chain regression on the latest baseline, the immutable-guest-replacement reminder, the transient Let's Encrypt CAA recheck failure, and the final successful retry.

verification.check: Authoritative exact-main replay from committed source
verification.observed: The final governed replay with `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` succeeded from committed source `eab6bac1ab76953ab0cef8da1c33f477befb73b4` in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-0.177.105-r5.txt` with play recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=264 changed=3 failed=0`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=40 changed=5 failed=0`, and `localhost : ok=24 changed=0 failed=0`.

Fresh public probes in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-ws-0279-mainline-public-probes-r2.txt` show `https://grist.lv3.org/status` returning `Grist server(home,docs,static) is alive.`, `https://grist.lv3.org/o/docs/` returning `HTTP/2 302` into the Keycloak auth flow, `https://docs.lv3.org/` and `https://changelog.lv3.org/` continuing to redirect into their oauth2-proxy sign-in flow, and the active Let's Encrypt certificate SAN set containing `grist.lv3.org`, `docs.lv3.org`, `changelog.lv3.org`, and `ops.lv3.org`.

Fresh host and guest snapshots in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-ws-0279-mainline-host-state-r2.txt` and `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-ws-0279-mainline-runtime-state-r2.txt` reconfirm `Debian-trixie-latest-amd64-base`, kernel `6.17.13-2-pve`, `pve-manager/9.1.6`, the guest-local `http://127.0.0.1:8484/status` endpoint answering the canonical alive string, and the live env split where `/opt/grist/grist.env` carries only static OIDC settings while `/run/lv3-secrets/grist/runtime.env` carries the session secret and OIDC client secret.

verification.check: Service redundancy rehearsal gate remains unproven
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/service_redundancy.py --check-live-apply --service grist` still reports `grist: declared R1 -> platform R1 -> implemented R0 [gate=unproven] (primary_only) standby=cold@docker-runtime-lv3`. The service is live and reachable, but no R1 rehearsal proof has been recorded yet.

notes: Grist first became true on production during the earlier workstream replay on platform version `0.130.60`; this canonical mainline receipt records the later synchronized latest-main verification on top of the `0.177.104 / 0.130.69` baseline and the resulting integrated platform baseline advance to `0.130.70`.

The earlier branch-local receipt `receipts/live-applies/2026-03-30-adr-0279-grist-live-apply.json` remains part of the audit trail, but `2026-03-30-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply` is now the canonical receipt for the `grist` capability on main.
The current public docs route returns an HTTP redirect into the Keycloak auth flow, and the exact-main runtime proof confirms the split static-plus-secret Grist env model on `docker-runtime-lv3`.
